meta.note: Data updated through May 10, 2026. Includes results of Round 2 Games 1–3. Lakers G3: Thunder 131, Lakers 108. OKC leads series 3-0.
meta.sitePagesNote: Site page copy can be edited per page: key is the HTML filename (relative to site root). With <html data-site-page="..."> and js/hydrate-site-pages.js included, use data-json-field for plain text (including <title>, meta description content) and data-json-html-field for trusted inline HTML. See austin-reaves-redemption-2026.html hero.*. Rebuild SEO fields via: node scripts/build-site-pages-json.mjs && node scripts/merge-site-pages-into-players.mjs

upcoming: About 170 more playoff assists to pass Magic Johnson's postseason assist record.
188 points away from the 8,500 playoff points milestone.
Roughly 170 rebounds to surpass Tim Duncan on the all-time playoff glass.

statusTemplate.template: LeBron has {{current}} playoff points, extending his all-time record.

players.seriesStatus: Lakers trail Thunder 0-3 after 108-131 Game 3 loss. Face potential sweep.

upcoming: About 310 more playoff points vaults him into the top ten on the career postseason scoring list.
Seventeen more assists ties Jerry West's playoff assist line from the classic-era leaderboards.
Fifty more triples before the next tier keeps the distance between him and every other shooter historically wide.

statusTemplate.template: Curry is {{gap}} playoff points away from passing Jerry West on the all-time postseason scoring list.

players.seriesStatus: Eliminated in First Round

upcoming: Under 200 points to pass another Hall-of-Fame guard on the playoff scoring ladder.
40 more triples moves him into the next cluster behind Curry-era shooters.
A healthy conference finals run could flip another rebounds-plus-points combo milestone.

statusTemplate.template: Durant is {{gap}} playoff points from climbing another spot on the all-time postseason scoring list.

players.seriesStatus: Eliminated in First Round

upcoming: One more big scoring series could lift him past several Hall-of-Fame forwards clustered around 2,350 playoff points.
Twenty-five boards in a deep run tightens the gap on the all-time playoff rebound list.
Three more playoff assists ties Ray Allen on the next rung of the all-time assist list.

statusTemplate.template: Giannis is {{gap}} playoff points away from passing Al Horford on the career postseason scoring list.

players.seriesStatus: Eliminated in First Round

upcoming: One more triple-double run tightens the gap with LeBron on the all-time playoff triple-double list.
Roughly twenty points clears the next cluster of forwards ahead of him on postseason scoring.
Eight more boards and he moves past Pau Gasol's playoff rebound total on the career list.

statusTemplate.many: Jokić is {{gap}} playoff assists away from matching Bill Russell's career playoff assist total.

players.seriesStatus: Eliminated in First Round

upcoming: Recorded his 2nd career playoff triple-double in Game 3 (27 PTS, 10 REB, 10 AST).
One more triple-double would tie Isiah Thomas' franchise record for a single postseason series.
Averaging 27.7 PPG and 9.7 APG in the series, leading the team in both.

statusTemplate.template: Cade is averaging {{avg}} points and {{ast}} assists in the series against the Cavaliers.

players.seriesStatus: Pistons lead Cavaliers 2-1. G1: 111-101, G2: 107-97 (5-game win streak), G3: lost 109-116. Cade had a 27-point triple-double but committed 8 turnovers.

milestones.name: Consecutive 20+ PTS playoff games
milestones.chasing: Sustaining the longest 20-point playoff streak of his career

upcoming: Has scored 20+ points in 8 consecutive playoff games, extending his personal record.
Posted 21 PTS, 7 REB, 2 STL, 2 BLK in Game 3, continuing his consistent two-way impact.

statusTemplate.template: Tobias Harris has now scored 20+ points in {{current}} consecutive playoff games.

players.seriesStatus: Pistons lead 2-1. Harris extended his 20-point streak to 8 games with a 21-point effort in Game 3.

upcoming: Exploded for 35 points and 10 rebounds in Game 3, becoming only the third Cavalier with a 35+10 playoff game.
Has raised his series scoring average to 30.0 PPG, fueling Cleveland's comeback push.

statusTemplate.template: Mitchell scored {{pts}} points in Game 3 to lead the Cavaliers to a crucial win.

players.seriesStatus: Cavaliers trail Pistons 1-2. G1: 101-111, G2: 97-107, G3: 116-109. Mitchell's 35-point, 10-rebound performance kept Cleveland alive.

milestones.name: Streak snapped in G3
milestones.chasing: Streak ended but still lead series 2-1

upcoming: 5-game win streak was snapped by the Cavaliers in Game 3, but the Pistons still control the series.
Last time Detroit had a 5-game playoff win streak was in 2008.

statusTemplate.template: Detroit's 5-game winning streak was snapped in Game 3. The Pistons still lead the series 2-1.

players.seriesStatus: Pistons lead Cavaliers 2-1. The 5-game streak ended with a 109-116 loss in Game 3.

upcoming: After a career-high 31 points in Game 2, Reaves had 17 points and 9 assists in Game 3, with scoring cooling but playmaking rising.
Lakers lost Game 3 108-131 and are down 0-3.

statusTemplate.template: After a 31-point Game 2, Reaves scored {{pts}} points with {{ast}} assists in Game 3.

players.seriesStatus: Lakers trail 0-3. Reaves averaging approximately 17 PTS and 6 AST in the playoffs.

upcoming: Averaging 19.0 PPG, 9.1 RPG, and 2.0 BPG in the playoffs, anchoring Oklahoma City's interior.
Has scored at least 18 points in 5 of 7 playoff games.
Contributed 18 PTS and 9 REB in Game 3, a 131-108 win over the Lakers.

statusTemplate.template: Chet is averaging {{avg}} points and {{reb}} rebounds in the 2026 playoffs.

players.seriesStatus: Thunder lead Lakers 3-0, undefeated in 7 playoff games. G3: 131-108 blowout win.

upcoming: Luka Doncic remains out, Lakers down 0-3 and one loss from elimination.
Ajay Mitchell tallied 24 PTS, 10 AST and 0 turnovers in Game 3, filling the backcourt seamlessly.

statusTemplate.template: Lakers are down 0-3 without Doncic. OKC is 7-0 in the playoffs.

Now 0-7 vs OKC this season (4 regular season, 3 playoffs).

players.seriesStatus: No team in NBA history has overcome a 0-3 deficit (0-157 all-time).

Averaging 20.5 PPG over his last 4 playoff games.

players.seriesStatus: Bridges has become the defensive anchor after Anunoby's injury.

players.displayName: Jalen Brunson: King of New York (2026)
upcoming: Scored 33 PTS with 9 AST in Game 3, pushing the Knicks to a 3-0 lead.
24 career playoff 30-point games as a Knick — the most in franchise history (Patrick Ewing had 18).
Averaging 31.3 PPG and 9.0 APG in the series, delivering a dominant two-way show.

statusTemplate.template: Brunson now has {{current}} career playoff games with 30+ points as a Knick.

players.seriesStatus: Knicks lead 76ers 3-0, one win away from a sweep.

upcoming: Returned in Game 3 with 18 PTS, 6 REB, 5 AST, 3 BLK but couldn't prevent a 0-3 hole.
Playing through ankle and hip injuries, far from full strength.

statusTemplate.template: Embiid returned in Game 3 with {{pts}} points, but the Sixers are down 0-3.

players.seriesStatus: 76ers trail 0-3, facing a sweep by the Knicks.

upcoming: After three straight 25+ point playoff wins, Game 3 was a 108-94 victory — still in control.
Now riding a 6-game playoff win streak.

players.seriesStatus: Knicks lead 76ers 3-0, one win from the Eastern Conference Finals.

upcoming: The 76ers haven't won a playoff series against the Knicks since 1983.
Trailing 0-3, the curse is virtually certain to extend to 43 years.

statusTemplate.template: The 76ers have lost {{current}} straight playoff series against the Knicks since 1983.

players.seriesStatus: 76ers down 0-3. Since 1983, Philadelphia is 0-4 in playoff series vs New York.

milestones.name: Shooting in G1
milestones.chasing: 29.4% FG, worst of his playoff career

upcoming: Blocked 12 shots in Game 1, setting a new NBA playoff single-game record.
Became only the third player in playoff history with a blocks triple-double (11 PTS, 15 REB, 12 BLK).
However, shot just 5-for-17 (0-8 3PT) — the worst postseason shooting of his career.

statusTemplate.template: Wembanyama set the NBA playoff record with {{current}} blocks in Game 1.

players.seriesStatus: Spurs lost Game 1 (102-104) despite Wembanyama's historic 12 blocks.

upcoming: Posted 39 points, 15 rebounds, 5 blocks on 72/60/83 shooting in Game 3.
Became the 4th player in NBA playoff history with 35+ PTS, 15+ REB, and 5+ BLK.
The only one among them to do it on 70%+ field goal shooting.

statusTemplate.template: Wembanyama's 39-15-5 Game 3 places him alongside Kareem, Hakeem, and Shaq.

players.seriesStatus: Spurs win 115-108, take 2-1 series lead. Game 3 was Wembanyama's coronation.

milestones.name: Game 3 rebounds
milestones.chasing: New playoff career high in boards

upcoming: In Game 1, returned early from a hyperextended left knee to score 18 off the bench in a surprise win.
Game 3: poured in 32 PTS, 14 REB, 6 AST, still unable to break Wembanyama's wall.
After Game 2, images of Edwards with ice packs on both knees underscored his gutsy effort.

statusTemplate.template: Despite {{pts}} points in Game 3, Edwards couldn't overcome Wembanyama's wall.

players.seriesStatus: Timberwolves trail Spurs 1-2. Edwards battles injury as Minnesota faces elimination risks.

players.displayName: Gobert vs. Wembanyama: Passing the Torch (2026)
upcoming: In Game 3, 33-year-old Gobert was dominated by 22-year-old Wembanyama: 39 PTS vs 13 PTS.
Wemby deployed a Hakeem Olajuwon spin fadeaway over Gobert three times in the 4th quarter.
This signified the formal torch-passing of French basketball from the 4-time DPOY to the unanimous DPOY.

statusTemplate.template: Wembanyama outscored Gobert {{gap}} points in Game 3, signaling a torch pass.

players.seriesStatus: Timberwolves trail 1-2. Gobert was thoroughly outplayed by Wembanyama on both ends in Game 3.

seo.title: Austin Reaves' 48-Hour Redemption: From 8-Point Disaster to 31-Point Playoff Career High
seo.description: The most dramatic bounce-back of the 2026 NBA Playoffs. Austin Reaves went from the worst playoff game by a Laker in 35 years to a career-high 31 points — in just 48 hours. Game 3 (May 9) added 17 points and 9 assists, but the Lakers fell behind 0-3.

hero.sub: Game 1: 8 points on 3-of-16 shooting. Game 2: 31 points on 10-of-16 shooting. Game 3 (May 9, home): 17 points and 9 assists — playmaking showed up even as the Lakers lost 108-131 and fell into an 0-3 hole.
hero.game3SummaryHtml: 17 PTS · 9 AST (team high) · follow-up to the 31-point explosion, with scoring cooling but table-setting up. The Lakers lost 108-131 and trail the Thunder 0-3 in the Western Semifinals.

seo.title: Cade Cunningham 2026 Playoff Milestones Tracker: The Rise of Detroit's Superstar
seo.description: Track Cade Cunningham's historic 2026 playoff run. From breaking Pistons franchise records to joining Oscar Robertson and Luka Doncic, see every milestone as Detroit chases the Eastern Conference Finals.

seo.title: Chet Holmgren 2026 Playoffs: DPOY-Level Defense & Historic Two-Way Dominance
seo.description: Chet Holmgren is anchoring the Thunder's title run with DPOY-level defense and near-190-club shooting. Every block, steal, and record from his dominant 2026 postseason vs the Lakers and beyond.

seo.title: Donovan Mitchell vs Pistons 2026 Playoffs | Scouting Report & Series Stats
seo.description: Donovan Mitchell and the Cavaliers vs the #1-seed Detroit Pistons in the 2026 Eastern Conference Semifinals. Series snapshot, head-to-head playoff numbers, and what Mitchell needs to flip the series.

seo.title: The Hero and the Unicorn Wall: Anthony Edwards' Gritty Return vs. Wembanyama's Empire — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: Anthony Edwards returned from a knee injury 9 days early, scored 18 points off the bench in Game 1, then poured in 32 points and 14 rebounds in Game 3. But Wembanyama's record 12 blocks in Game 1 and 39-point Game 3 masterpiece built an unbreakable wall.

seo.title: The Prodigy and the Master: Rudy Gobert's Darkest Hour vs. Victor Wembanyama — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: Rudy Gobert has 4 DPOY trophies and just locked down Nikola Jokic. But in the 2026 Western Semifinals, his 22-year-old French protégé Victor Wembanyama is destroying him — 39 points on 72% shooting, a Hakeem Olajuwon spin fadeaway over the master, and a 2-1 series deficit.

seo.title: NBA Playoffs Milestone Tracker | Player Stories & Record Chases
seo.description: Search NBA playoff stars by name, open every story page from one hub, and follow live milestone chase cards for LeBron James, Stephen Curry, Kevin Durant, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Nikola Jokić, and more.

seo.title: Jalen Brunson: The King of New York — 24 Playoff 30-Point Games & Counting | 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: Jalen Brunson has more 30-point playoff games than any player in Knicks history. From his NBA-record 15-point streak to joining the MJ & Kareem club, track every milestone of the King of New York's 2026 playoff dominance vs. the 76ers.

seo.title: Joel Embiid's Philly Nightmare: The Knicks Takeover, the Injuries, and the 0-3 Abyss — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: Joel Embiid begged 76ers fans not to sell their tickets. 59% of Game 3 ticket sales came from New York. Knicks fans booed him at his own free-throw line. Now he's down 0-3 with a sprained ankle and sore hip.

seo.title: 119 Points of Destruction: The Knicks' Record-Shattering Three-Game Playoff Run — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: The Knicks are the first team in NBA history to win three straight playoff games by 25+ points. 29, 51, and 39-point wins. Largest three-game margin in playoff history.

seo.title: Lakers' Biggest Playoff Underdog Challenge in Franchise History: +950 vs. Thunder — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: The Lakers face the largest playoff underdog odds in franchise history since 1988. +950 series price, 15.5-point Game 1 spread, LeBron's biggest career underdog. Now down 0-3 after Game 3.

seo.title: The Changing of the Guard: LeBron James' 300th Playoff Game vs. OKC's Rising Dynasty — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: The 2026 Western Conference Semifinals is more than a series — it's the passing of the torch. LeBron James reached 300 playoff games as the Thunder's young dynasty rolled to a 2-0 lead; after Game 3, OKC is up 3-0.

seo.title: 2026 NBA Playoffs: Lakers Without Luka Doncic vs. Thunder's Unstoppable Depth — A Two-Sided Story
seo.description: The Lakers are missing scoring champion Luka Doncic, and the Thunder are showing why they're the deepest team in the NBA. After Game 3, OKC bench and depth continue to overwhelm.

seo.title: Mikal Bridges: The Final Piece — From KD Trade Asset to Knicks Championship Puzzle | 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: Mikal Bridges was the centerpiece of the Kevin Durant trade. Now he's locking down Tyrese Maxey, silencing all doubters, and proving he's the missing piece to New York's championship puzzle.

seo.title: Detroit Pistons Playoff Records Tracker 2026: From 14 Wins to Championship Chase
seo.description: Track every Detroit Pistons franchise playoff record in 2026. From ending the 18-year series win drought to Cade Cunningham breaking Isiah Thomas' scoring record.

seo.title: 42 Years of Pain: The 76ers' Playoff Curse Against the Knicks — 1983 to 2026
seo.description: The 76ers haven't won a playoff series against the Knicks since 1983. Four straight Knicks series wins. 9 all-time playoff meetings. A 42-year curse.

seo.title: Tobias Harris 2026 Playoff Scoring Streak: 7 Straight 20-Point Games and Counting
seo.description: Track Tobias Harris' incredible 2026 playoff scoring streak. From zero consecutive 20-point games in the regular season to 7 straight in the playoffs — now at 8 games and counting.

seo.title: Wembanyama's 12 Blocks Not Enough: Spurs' Historic Defensive Night Ends in 104-102 Game 1 Loss — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: Victor Wembanyama set the NBA playoff record with 12 blocks and became only the 3rd player ever with a blocks triple-double. But the Spurs lost 104-102 to the Timberwolves.

seo.title: The Coronation of Wemby: 39 Points, Historic 35/15/5 Club & the Game 3 Masterclass — 2026 NBA Playoffs
seo.description: Victor Wembanyama delivered 39 points, 15 rebounds, and 5 blocks on 72% shooting in Game 3. He joined Kareem, Hakeem, and Shaq as the only players in NBA history with 35/15/5 in a playoff game.
